spec.goal: Implement Symphony-aligned same-thread continuation turns for Maestro-owned runs so one active issue can advance through multiple turns in the same thread and workspace under an explicit repo-owned turn budget.
success_criteria: The workflow contract accepts a repo-owned max_turns setting with the current single-turn behavior preserved by default.
The runtime can continue an active issue in the same thread and workspace across multiple turns without reopening a fresh issue lane for every turn.

Between continuation turns, Maestro re-checks tracker state and orchestration guards and stops cleanly when the issue becomes inactive, the turn budget is exhausted, or existing completion or failure rules fire.
Specs, operator docs, and targeted tests cover continuation-turn behavior, stop conditions, and defaulting.
constraints: Use openai/symphony README and SPEC as the only external design standard.
Keep the lifecycle boundary at the current PR-backed In Review handoff; do not fold post-review automation into this issue.
Preserve current behavior when max_turns is unset or set to 1.
Use cargo make for repo-native verification commands.

tasks.title: Pin the continuation-turn contract
tasks.objective: Capture the exact local code paths and spec surfaces that currently enforce one-thread-one-turn execution, then translate the relevant Symphony continuation semantics into a concrete Maestro contract for max_turns, same-thread reuse, and stop conditions.
outputs: A stable implementation target for max_turns and continuation-turn boundaries
A confirmed list of local files that must change
verification: The target contract distinguishes same-thread continuation from retry-driven fresh dispatch.
The target contract keeps the current In Review handoff boundary unchanged.

tasks.title: Implement same-thread continuation turns
tasks.objective: Extend the workflow and runtime so one issue run can reuse the same app-server thread and workspace across multiple turns, with explicit turn-budget handling and guard checks between turns.
outputs: Updated app-server run loop and orchestrator logic
Updated tests for same-thread continuation and stop conditions
verification: Default behavior remains single-turn when max_turns is unset or 1.
Continuation runs stop when the issue becomes inactive, the turn budget is exhausted, or existing completion or failure rules fire.

tasks.title: Sync docs and validate the lane
tasks.objective: Update the runtime and operator docs to reflect continuation turns, then run the repo-native verification stack and record the resulting evidence.
outputs: Verification evidence recorded in the saved plan

triggers: Symphony alignment requires changing the continuation model shape or stop conditions materially.
The implementation needs lifecycle semantics beyond the current In Review boundary.
The saved task graph proves insufficient once the runtime hooks for same-thread continuation are inspected in detail.

evidence: 2026-03-19: Created clone-backed lane .workspaces/XY-157 because the root checkout still has a user-owned WORKFLOW.md modification.
2026-03-19: Baseline cargo make fmt-check passed in .workspaces/XY-157.

2026-03-19: Probed the current runtime and app-server surfaces; execute_app_server_run_inner still performs exactly one thread/start and one turn/start per run, while current docs describe retry-driven continuation rather than same-thread turn continuation.
2026-03-19: Reconfirmed openai/symphony README.md and SPEC.md as the sole external design standard for the continuation-turn contract.
2026-03-19: Added execution.max_turns to the workflow contract with a default of 1, explicit validation against zero, and checked-in documentation/examples covering the new setting.

2026-03-19: Extended the app-server run loop to support bounded same-thread multi-turn execution, with continuation classification delegated through the dynamic tool handler and guarded between-turn continuation checks.

2026-03-19: Updated tracker completion signaling so nonterminal same-thread continuation is distinct from terminal review-handoff or needs-attention completion, preserving XY-162 terminal finalization requirements.
2026-03-19: Updated Maestro runtime, app-server, workflow, and pilot docs to describe same-thread continuation turns and clean continuation-boundary exits.

2026-03-19: review-prepare found and fixed one default-behavior regression: max_turns = 1 now preserves the pre-XY-157 invalid-completion failure path for nonterminal turns instead of silently converting them into clean continuation exits.

2026-03-19: After that self-review repair, cargo make fmt, cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, cargo make test, cargo test continuation -- --nocapture, and git diff --check all passed in .workspaces/XY-157.

2026-03-19: CI reproduced a merge-base drift against current origin/main: RunSummary gained issue_state and retry_project_slug on main after the lane branched from 5f261f2, so the continuation-boundary early return had to be rebased onto origin/main and switched to run_summary_from_issue_run(...) instead of a stale hand-written initializer.
2026-03-19: After rebasing XY-157 onto origin/main, cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, and cargo make test all passed again on the repaired head.

2026-03-19: review-repair for head 07633a8 reconciled the continuation prompt contract, rejected turn-1 clean yields when the startup tracker transition never happened, compared continuation eligibility against the resolved retry project slug, and refreshed tracker issue labels before cross-turn label writes.

2026-03-19: Fresh repair verification passed on the current working tree: cargo test continuation -- --nocapture, cargo test label_add_refreshes_issue_snapshot_before_merging_label_ids -- --nocapture, cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, cargo make test, and git diff --check.

2026-03-19: review-repair for head 748f6e3 found two new continuation-boundary gaps on the current PR head: local tracker writes could turn nonterminal continuation into a silent non-active exit, and direct run_once callers treated continuation boundaries like completed runs.

2026-03-19: The current repair batch now rejects clean continuation after local tracker writes that leave the issue non-active without a terminal path, and it surfaces direct run_once continuation boundaries explicitly via RunSummary.continuation_pending plus a paused run message. Fresh verification on the repaired diff passed: cargo test turn_classification_rejects_opt_out_label_without_terminal_path -- --nocapture, cargo test turn_classification_rejects_non_in_progress_transition_without_terminal_path -- --nocapture, cargo test format_run_once_summary_surfaces_continuation_boundaries -- --nocapture, cargo make fmt, cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, cargo make test, and git diff --check.

2026-03-19: review-repair for head d66c334 found one remaining prompt-level P1: the initial-turn prompt still allowed nonterminal yield even when execution.max_turns stayed at the single-turn default. The repair now gates that allowance on max_turns > 1 in build_developer_instructions and build_user_input, adds single-turn and multi-turn prompt coverage, reruns cargo make lint-fix to clear vstyle findings, confirms the known shared_dispatch_slots_honor_configured_limit_across_process_local_stores test passes on targeted rerun after a transient full-suite failure, and then reruns cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, cargo make test, and git diff --check successfully.

2026-03-19: review-repair for head ca890e2 found one remaining P2 in TrackerToolBridge: continuation-blocking writes could fall back to the stale pre-run issue snapshot when refresh_issues() returned no current issue. The repair now makes label writes fail fast when no refreshed snapshot exists and treats missing refreshed snapshots as continuation-blocking in classify_turn_completion instead of allowing a clean continuation boundary.

2026-03-19: Fresh verification for the ca890e2 repair passed after updating the affected label/manual-attention tests to provide explicit current tracker snapshots: cargo test label_add_fails_when_refresh_returns_no_snapshot -- --nocapture, cargo test turn_classification_rejects_continuation_blocking_write_when_refresh_returns_no_snapshot -- --nocapture, cargo test adds_allowed_workflow_label -- --nocapture, cargo test completion_disposition_allows_manual_attention_exit_without_review_handoff -- --nocapture, cargo test manual_attention_requires_explanatory_comment -- --nocapture, cargo test completion_disposition_rejects_conflicting_review_handoff_and_manual_attention -- --nocapture, cargo test terminal_finalize_accepts_matching_manual_attention_path -- --nocapture, cargo make fmt, cargo make lint-fix, cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, cargo make test, and git diff --check all passed. One intermediate cargo make test rerun hit the known transient state::tests::shared_dispatch_slots_honor_configured_limit_across_process_local_stores failure in the plain cargo test phase; the targeted rerun passed immediately and the subsequent full cargo make test rerun passed end-to-end.
